title: Extend Object Field Model with Salesforce FieldDefinition Metadata and Change Tracking
story: As a DataTools Pro user viewing the Object Dictionary, I want to see additional Salesforce field metadata (such as calculated status, security classification, and last modified date) and view a history of field changes, so that I can understand field characteristics and track when fields were last modified in Salesforce.
description:
This enhancement extends the object field data model by querying Salesforce's FieldDefinition SOQL endpoint to capture additional metadata attributes not available through the standard describe API. A secondary SOQL query is daisy-chained after the existing describe call, and the results are merged into the existing field records by matching on field name.

### New Fields Added to the Data Model
- `IsCalculated` — whether the field is a formula/calculated field
- `ExtraTypeInfo` — additional type metadata
- `IsPolymorphicForeignKey` — polymorphic lookup indicator
- `LastModifiedById` — who last modified the field in Salesforce
- `LastModifiedDate` — when the field was last modified in Salesforce
- `ReferenceTo` — target object for lookup/reference fields
- `SecurityClassification` — field security classification

### Change Tracking
Each time an object is refreshed, the system calculates `MAX(LastModifiedDate)` across all fields and logs it. This enables tracking:
- Last time DataTools Pro refreshed the object
- Last time any field was modified in Salesforce
- Whether anything changed since the last refresh

### UX for Field History
A 'View All History' icon was added to the object detail header showing a table of all field changes. Additionally, individual field rows display a history icon (visible only when that field has been modified) linking to the field-level change history. The product owner requested suppressing two specific attributes from the UX to keep the interface simple.
acceptance_criteria: Refreshing an object fetches additional metadata via SOQL FieldDefinition query
New metadata fields (IsCalculated, ExtraTypeInfo, IsPolymorphicForeignKey, LastModifiedById, LastModifiedDate, ReferenceTo, SecurityClassification) are stored in the existing field table
Existing field records are updated (not duplicated) when SOQL results are merged
New fields from the SOQL query that do not exist in the describe results are inserted
A 'View All History' icon in the object header opens a table of all field changes
Individual field rows show a history icon only when that field has modification history
Clicking a field-level history icon shows that specific field's change history
The last refresh timestamp is recorded for each object
ux_changes: Added 'View All History' icon to object header and per-field history icons on rows with modifications. History tables display field change records. Two metadata attributes were suppressed from the UX per product owner direction to keep the interface simple.
middleware_changes: Added a secondary SOQL query to FieldDefinition after the describe API call. Results are matched by field name — existing records are updated, new fields are inserted. MAX(LastModifiedDate) is calculated on each refresh.
data_model_changes: Extended the object fields table with columns: IsCalculated, ExtraTypeInfo, IsPolymorphicForeignKey, LastModifiedById, LastModifiedDate, ReferenceTo, SecurityClassification. Added refresh timestamp tracking per object.
api_changes: New SOQL query: SELECT DataType, Description, DeveloperName, DurableId, EntityDefinitionId, ExtraTypeInfo, Id, IsCalculated, IsNameField, IsPolymorphicForeignKey, Label, LastModifiedById, LastModifiedDate, Length, MasterLabel, QualifiedApiName, ReferenceTargetField, ReferenceTo, RelationshipName, SecurityClassification, ServiceDataTypeId, ValueTypeId FROM FieldDefinition WHERE EntityDefinitionId = '{objectName}'.
implementation_notes: The existing describe API (`/services/data/v56.0/sobjects/{objectName}/describe`) returns field names but not the additional FieldDefinition attributes. The SOQL query supplements this. Both results are merged in a single table rather than using a join, for query performance. A future DataTools Native Salesforce object was mentioned as a longer-term solution for change log tracking. An 'Audit' tab concept was discussed for housing change tracking features.
comment_summary: Developer initially confused about where to use the SOQL query (vs describe API). Product owner clarified it's a SOQL query, not a REST endpoint. Developer implemented the two-pass approach (describe + SOQL update) and built field history UX with both object-level and field-level views. Product owner requested suppressing two metadata attributes from the UX and mentioned a future DataTools Native Salesforce object for change log tracking.
